questions.sentence: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) is one of the most common and dangerous security vulnerabilities in modern web applications.
An XSS attack occurs when a malicious actor injects client-side scripts into a webpage viewed by other users.
To prevent XSS, developers must follow strict security protocols, such as 'Sanitizing' all user-controlled inputs to remove potentially harmful code.
Furthermore, using a 'Content Security Policy' (CSP) provides an additional layer of defense by restricting the sources from which scripts can be loaded and executed.

Modern frameworks like React and Vue have built-in protections that escape data by default, but developers must still stay vigilant and avoid patterns that bypass these safety measures, such as using 'dangerouslySetInnerHTML'.
